zh.lifeSummary: 當Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX於1264年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特阿生頓時，他的父親Sir Knight Matthew Somerset Furneaux53歲，母親Isabel De Avenell34歲。他於1284年與Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe在英國薩默塞特內特萊可姆結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1317年在英國英格蘭薩默塞特吉爾福享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX于1264年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特阿生頓时，他的父亲Sir Knight Matthew Somerset Furneaux53岁，母亲Isabel De Avenell34岁。他于1284年与Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe在英国薩默塞特內特萊可姆结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1317年在英国英格兰薩默塞特吉爾福享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: FURNEAUX
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from either of two places in Normandy called Fourneaux ‘(the) furnaces’, one in Manche, the other in Calvados. Compare   Furnace   and   Furnell  .

variants.name: Furney
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Furneaux  . It may sometimes have been confused with   Furness  ; the two names have variants in common (see   Furnace  ).

variants.name: Fornell
meanings: English: variant of Furneaux , or a nickname from Middle English fornel ‘small oven or furnace’.

variants.name: Furnace
meanings: English:
(Cumbria and Durham): variant of   Furness  .
(of Norman origin): sometimes a variant of   Furneaux  .

variants.name: Furnell
meanings: English:
variant of   Furneaux  .
alternatively, perhaps a variant of   Farnell  . Compare   Funnell  .

nameMeanings.name: Matthew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name of the Christian evangelist, author of the first gospel in the New Testament. His name is a form of the Hebrew name Mattathia, meaning ‘gift of God’, which is fairly common in the Old Testament, being rendered in the Authorized Version in a number of different forms: Mattan(i)ah, Mattatha(h), Mattithiah, Mattathias, and so on. In the Authorized Version, the evangelist is regularly referred to as Matthew, while the apostle chosen to replace Judas Iscariot is distinguished as Matthias . A related name from the same Hebrew roots, but reversed, is Jonathan . Throughout the English-speaking world Matthew has been particularly popular since the 1970s.

variants.name: Mathew
meanings: Variant spelling of   Matthew .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of George .

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Knight
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Ashington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Especially U.S.: transferred use of the surname of the first president of the United States, George Washington ( 1732–99 ), whose family came originally from Northamptonshire in England. They had been established in Virginia since 1656 . The surname in this case is derived from the village of Washington in Co. Durham (now Tyne and Wear), so called from Old English Wassingtūn ‘settlement associated with Wassa’.

nameMeanings.name: Somerset
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

habitational name from the English county of Somerset, which takes its name from Somerton in Somerset (see Summerton ) + Old English sǣte ‘settlers, dwellers’. The Somerset family who are Dukes of Beaufort are descended from the Plantagenets. The surname was assumed by Charles Somerset (1460–1520), illegitimate son of Henry Beaufort, Duke of Somerset. The latter was himself descended illegitimately from Edward III through John of Gaunt. A later member of the family was Fitzroy Somerset (1788–1855), who, as Lord Raglan, was the commander of British forces in the Crimea, one of the men responsible for the charge of the Light Brigade.

possibly a habitational name from one or more places called with Old Norse sumar ‘summer’ + sætr ‘shieling, hill pasture’, such as Summerseat in Elton (Lancashire), but decisive northern early bearers are lacking.

variants.name: Summerton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Somerton, particularly in Oxfordshire and Somerset, but also in Lincolnshire and Norfolk, named with Old English sumor ‘summer’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Summersett
meanings: English (Yorkshire): variant of   Somerset  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX was born in 1264, in Ashington, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Knight Matthew Somerset Furneaux, was 53 and his mother, Isabel De Avenell, was 34. He married Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe in 1284, in Nettlecombe, Somerset, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He died in 1317, in Kilve, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX est né en 1264, à Ashington, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Knight Matthew Somerset Furneaux, avait 53 ans et sa mère, Isabel De Avenell, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe en 1284, à Nettlecombe, Somerset, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé en 1317, à Kilve, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX 1264 in Ashington, Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Knight Matthew Somerset Furneaux 53 und seine Mutter Isabel De Avenell 34. Er hat 1284 in Nettlecombe, Somerset, England Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Er starb 1317 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Kilve, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX è nato il nel 1264 nel Ashington, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Knight Matthew Somerset Furneaux, aveva 53 anni e il suo madre, Isabel De Avenell, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe nel 1284, nel Nettlecombe, Somerset, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1317, nel Kilve, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUXが1264年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトアシュイントンで生まれた時，父Sir Knight Matthew Somerset Furneauxは53歳，母Isabel De Avenellは34歳であった。1284年にイギリスソメルセトネットレコンベでMaud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1317年にイギリスイギリスソメルセトキルブで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX(이)가 1264년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 애신그턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Knight Matthew Somerset Furneaux(은)는 53세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel De Avenell(은)는 34세였다. 그는 1284년 영국 서머세트 네틀렉브에서 Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1317년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 킬브에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX nasceu em 1264, em Ashington, Somerset, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Knight Matthew Somerset Furneaux, tinha 53 anos e sua mãe, Isabel De Avenell, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe em 1284, em Nettlecombe, Somerset, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX родился в 1264 г. в местности ашингтон, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Knight Matthew Somerset Furneaux было 53 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel De Avenell, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe в 1284 г. в местности неттлекомб, сомирсет, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Он умер в 1317 г. в местности килв, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matthew Sir Knight of Ashington Somerset de FURNEAUX nació en 1264, en Ashington, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Knight Matthew Somerset Furneaux, tenía 53 años y su mamá, Isabel De Avenell, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Maud De Raleigh Of Nettlecombe en 1284, en Nettlecombe, Somerset, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 4 hijas. Murió en 1317, en Kilve, Somerset, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 53 años.
